title: Audio Tags Guide - Eleven V3
description: Master expressive speech synthesis with audio tags

sections.title: What are Audio Tags?
sections.content: Audio tags are special markers in [square brackets] that control how Eleven V3 delivers speech. They add emotions, vocal sounds, and expressive elements to make your audio more natural and engaging.
sections.tip: Audio tags work best with the Stability slider set to 'Creative' or 'Natural' mode.

sections.title: Emotions & Delivery
sections.description: Control the emotional tone of speech

tags.example: [happy] I just got the best news today!
[sad] I really thought things would be different.
[excited] You won't believe what just happened!
[angry] This is completely unacceptable!
[whisper] Don't tell anyone, but I saw everything.
[sarcastic] Oh sure, because that worked so well last time.
[curious] What do you think is behind that door?
[thoughtful] I wonder if we made the right choice.
[surprised] Wait, you're actually here?
[annoyed] I've already told you three times.
[cautiously] Are you sure this is safe?
[cheerfully] Good morning everyone!
[mischievously] I have an idea...
[appalled] You did WHAT?
[elated] We actually did it! We won!

sections.title: Non-Verbal Sounds
sections.description: Add realistic vocal sounds and reactions

tags.example: [laughs] That's the funniest thing I've heard all day!
[laughs harder] Stop, I can't breathe!
[chuckles] You always know how to make me smile.
[giggling] That tickles!
[wheezing] I can't... stop...
Well, [sighs] I guess we have to start over.
[exhales] Finally, it's done.
[inhales deeply] Okay, let's do this.
[clears throat] Ladies and gentlemen, welcome.
[groaning] Not another meeting...
[crying] I just miss them so much.
[snorts] As if that would ever work.
[gulps] You want me to do what?
[swallows] This is harder than I thought.
I think [short pause] we should reconsider.
And the winner is [long pause] you!

punctuationTips.description: Creates natural pauses and trailing off
punctuationTips.example: I thought we could... never mind.

sections.title: Special & Experimental
sections.warning: These tags are experimental and may not work consistently with all voices.

tags.description: Applies an accent (replace X with accent name)
tags.example: [strong French accent] Bonjour, my friend!
[sings] Happy birthday to you!
[woo] Let's go!

sections.title: Sound Effects
sections.warning: Sound effect tags work inconsistently and are best for creative experimentation.

tags.example: [applause] Thank you all for coming!
[gunshot] What was that?!
[explosion] Get down!
[clapping] Great job everyone!

sections.title: Combining Tags
sections.description: Use multiple tags for complex emotions

examples.text: [excited] Oh my god! [gulps] Is this really happening?
[sarcastic] Wow, what a surprise. [sighs] I totally didn't see that coming.
examples.explanation: Layers sarcasm with an exasperated sigh

examples.text: [thoughtful] I've been thinking and... [long pause] [sad] I think we need to talk.
examples.explanation: Builds tension with pause before emotional shift

examples.text: [cheerfully] So I walked right up to her and said— [clears throat] [cautiously] Actually, maybe I shouldn't tell this story.

speakers.text: [excitedly] Sam! Have you tried the new feature?
[curiously] Just got it! The clarity is amazing.

examples.title: Interruption Pattern
examples.tip: Use em dashes (—) to show cut-off speech

speakers.text: [elated] Yes! [laughs] I'm so glad you knew!

tips.title: Match voice to tags
tips.description: A whispering voice won't suddenly shout well. Choose voices that match your intended emotional range.

tips.title: Use Creative stability for expression
tips.description: Set the Stability slider to 'Creative' or 'Natural' for best tag responsiveness. 'Robust' mode reduces tag effectiveness.

tips.title: Don't overuse tags
tips.description: Too many tags can make speech feel unnatural. Use them at key emotional moments.

tips.title: Place tags strategically
tips.description: Tags work best at the beginning of phrases or right before the affected words.

tips.title: Test and iterate
tips.description: Different voices respond differently to tags. Experiment to find what works best.

tips.title: Combine with punctuation
tips.description: Use CAPS for emphasis, ellipses for pauses, and em dashes for interruptions alongside tags.
